findings.title: Accept Plan silently deletes ALL numbered days — wiping day journals, plan text, and permanently destroying day-attached photos/documents from disk
findings.rootCause: onAcceptPlan (useAiPlan.ts:450-456) does `existingNumbered = STATE.tripDays.filter(d => d.tripId === activeTrip.id && d.dayNumber > 0)` then `existingNumbered.forEach(d => deleteDayOnServer(d.id))` — it deletes EVERY numbered day regardless of origin, not just AI-created ones (there is no source/provenance check on days, unlike markedPlaces which has dropAITaggedPlaces). deleteDayOnServer hits DELETE /api/days/<id>, whose handler (days.py:264-352) runs a delete-cascade: it collects upload paths for trip-level photos_json/documents_json items with `dayId == day_id`, DROPS them from the JSON arrays, and calls delete_upload_files(upload_paths, owner_id) (days.py:351-352) which unlinks the files from disk. Photos are attached to days with the trip-day id as dayId (tripMediaModals.ts:429,782,953), and journal text is written to day.notes (journalingModal.ts:42). Because ItineraryOutput's `accepted` flag is component-local useState (ItineraryOutput.tsx:87) it resets on every remount, and the itinerary is re-shown from activeTrip.aiPlan on return to /ai — so the user can navigate away and back and re-accept, and a first-time AI user with auto-scaffolded days (modals/trip.ts:214, dayNumber starts at 1) loses everything on their FIRST accept.

findings.title: AI itinerary renders as EMPTY cards (and Accept writes '[object Object]') when the server has no Google Maps key — the documented 'graceful no-op' path is broken for the food/sights schema
findings.rootCause: When no Maps key is configured, _enrich_itinerary returns the itinerary UNCHANGED (integrations.py:307-308), so for the new food/sights schema the meal/sight objects keep their raw Gemini shape {name, why, fact} and never get a `text` field (text is only added by _enrich_one at integrations.py:339 on the enrichment path). The frontend renderer renderSlotItem keys ENTIRELY off `text`: `const text = String(v.text || ''); if (!text) return '';` (slots.ts:122-123) — so an object with `name` but no `text` renders nothing. MealBlock's has-content guard checks `p.text || p.name` (ItineraryOutput.tsx:291) so it thinks there IS content and calls renderRestaurantCard, which then emits an empty string. On Accept, itemToText (slots.ts:231-235) falls through to `String(item ?? '')` for an object lacking a string `text`, yielding '[object Object]', which flattenMealForTextarea writes into the day plan. The legacy items[] string schema is unaffected (strings render as bullets) — only the current food/sights schema is broken without a Maps key.

findings.title: A saved BYO Gemini key permanently disables the shared host-pool fallback — a user with an exhausted personal key is stuck with no way to generate
findings.rootCause: runGenerate always sends `gemini_key: (STATE.geminiApiKey || '').trim()` on every request (useAiPlan.ts:361). Server-side, if the key matches the shape regex, using_byo=true and `keys_to_try = [(0, user_key)]` ONLY (integrations.py:673-677) — the else-branch that builds the host pool is skipped. On a quota error from the BYO key, slot 0 is exempt from cooldown and the loop simply exhausts with no host-pool attempt (integrations.py:871-873). There is no 'fall back to host pool if BYO fails' path.

findings.title: Re-running Accept Plan orphans manually day-assigned places (dangling dayId) — they silently drop out of their day and fall into 'Unsorted'
findings.rootCause: Each Accept Plan run mints brand-new day ids (`'day_' + Date.now() + '_' + idx`, useAiPlan.ts:466) and deletes the old days. addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified only re-stamps the dayId of places that appear in the new plan (markedPlaces.ts:259). A manual place whose placeId isn't in the new plan retains its old dayId, which now references a deleted day. The day-detail and media-grouping code filter by `dayId === day.id` (dayDetailModal.ts:354) and bucket unmatched dayIds as '__orphan__'/Unsorted (tripMediaModals.ts:97), so the assignment is effectively lost.

findings.title: numDays has no client-side upper bound — request a 31+ day range and the result heading lies ('60-Day Itinerary' but only 30 days render/accept)
findings.rootCause: Client computes numDays = Math.max(1, Math.round(diff)+1) with NO upper clamp (useAiPlan.ts:291-294) and stores it as activeTrip.aiNumDays (line 328). The heading renders savedNumDays = activeTrip.aiNumDays (AI.tsx:78 → ItineraryOutput.tsx:116, key ai.resultHeading '{numDays}-Day {country} Itinerary'). The server clamps num_days = max(1, min(30, ...)) (integrations.py:602), so the actual itinerary length is capped at 30 while the heading shows the uncapped client value.

findings.title: Hardcoded English strings on the AI surface (BYO key status, card aria-labels, 'unverified' chip + tooltip) break i18n for es/fr/pt users
findings.rootCause: These strings are inline literals instead of t() lookups. Notably useAiPlan.ts keyStatus uses t('ai.keyStatusEmpty') for the empty case (line 218) but hardcodes the 'saved' and 'format off' branches (lines 221-225). slots.ts:180/209 build raw HTML with English aria-label/title/chip text; AIUsageCard.tsx:96 and TodoListPanel.tsx:373-374 hardcode aria-label/title.
